interview_transcript.content: "Good afternoon, Ms. Patel. I'm Officer Davis. I'm following up on the incident reported earlier today at 1452 Santiago Drive regarding a possible protection order violation involving your ex-boyfriend. I know going over these events can be incredibly stressful, so please take all the time you need. We're just here to get the facts down for the official report. Whenever you're ready, please start from the beginning and walk me through exactly what happened."

(taking a deep breath) Oh, thank you, Officer Davis. I... I think I'm ready. It's just been a really scary and overwhelming day. (pausing for a moment) Okay, so... Javier, my ex-boyfriend, he showed up at my house this morning. I had a protection order against him, and I thought he was supposed to stay away from me. But he just... he just showed up. He was angry, and he started yelling at me. He said I was his, and that I couldn't leave him. (my voice starts to shake) He got really close to me, and I could see the anger in his eyes. He was holding a kitchen knife, and he... he threatened me with it. He said he would hurt me if I didn't do what he wanted. (tearfully) And then... and then he grabbed me and started choking me. I couldn't breathe, and I thought... I thought he was going to kill me. (sobbing) I'm so scared of him, Officer Davis. I don't know what he's capable of.

"Ms. Patel, I hear you, and your fear is completely justified. What you just described is an incredibly traumatic and dangerous experience, but I want to assure you that you are safe right now. Because he forced his way in, used a weapon, and choked you, we are looking at much more serious charges than just a protection order violation, including aggravated assault.

To help my team locate him immediately and secure the scene, I need to pin down a few specific facts from you.

First, I strongly advise that we have EMS come in to check your neck. Strangulation can cause severe internal injuries that aren't always immediately visible, and having a medical record will also strengthen the case against him. Please let me know if you will consent to a medical evaluation.

Second, regarding the kitchen knife he threatened you with, I need you to clarify if he brought that weapon with him to your house, or if he grabbed it from your own kitchen.

Finally, please tell me exactly how the assault ended and how he left the property, including whether he fled on foot or if you saw him drive away in a specific vehicle."

(sniffling) Oh, thank you, Officer Davis. Just knowing that you're taking this seriously and that you're going to help me... it means a lot. (taking a deep breath) Okay, yes... I consent to the medical evaluation. I didn't think about internal injuries, but it makes sense. My neck is still sore, and my throat hurts when I swallow. (touching my neck gingerly)

Regarding the knife... I think he grabbed it from my kitchen. I don't remember seeing him bring it with him, but I could be wrong. I was so scared, and everything happened so fast. But I do remember seeing him walk into the kitchen and then coming back with the knife. It was one of my own kitchen knives, and it was... it was really terrifying to see him holding it like that.

As for how the assault ended... (shuddering at the memory) I think I managed to push him away from me, or maybe he just let me go. I don't know. But I remember running to the living room and locking myself in. I called 911 from there, and I stayed on the line until I heard the sirens. I didn't see him leave the property, but I heard the front door slam shut. I didn't see a car, but I did hear the sound of footsteps outside, and then it was quiet. I was too scared to look out the window, so I don't know if he had a car or if he ran away. (starting to tear up again) I'm just so glad he's gone, and I'm safe now.

"I’m sorry, Leila. I’m moving too fast. Let’s take a second. You’re doing great, and the most important thing is that you’re safe and the medics are on their way to help you.

You mentioned he went into the kitchen to get that knife. Can you point out to me exactly where he grabbed it from? I want to make sure my team doesn't touch that area so we can get his prints off the counter or the drawer."

(exhaling slowly) Okay, thank you, Officer Davis. I appreciate your concern. (composing myself) Yes, the kitchen... it's just through that doorway. (pointing) If you go into the kitchen, the knife block is on the counter, right next to the sink. I think he grabbed the knife from the block. It's a wooden block with five slots, and the knife he used was the biggest one, the chef's knife. (shuddering at the memory) I remember seeing him pull it out of the block and then... (trailing off)

But, yes, please be careful. I don't know if he touched anything else in the kitchen, but I think he might have touched the counter or the sink when he was getting the knife. And maybe the drawer handle, too, because I think he opened the drawer to... (pausing, trying to remember) no, wait, I'm not sure about the drawer. Just the counter and the knife block, I'm pretty sure. (looking at you anxiously) Will that help? Will his fingerprints be on the counter?

"That helps immensely, Leila. Yes, the knife block and the counter are great surfaces for lifting prints. Even if he didn't realize it, he likely left behind exactly the kind of evidence we need to put him away for a long time. My team will be very careful with that area.

I have one more question for you right now, and then I'll let the medics take over.

**Do you recall if he was wearing any gloves, or were his hands bare when he grabbed the knife and when he... when he put his hands on you?**"

(shuddering at the memory) No, he wasn't wearing gloves. His hands were bare. I remember feeling his skin on mine, and it was... it was so cold and rough. (shivering) When he grabbed the knife, I saw his bare hand wrap around the handle, and when he... when he choked me, his hands were around my neck, and I could feel his fingers digging into my skin. (touching my neck again, wincing at the memory) It was so painful, and it's still sore now.

(looking up at you) I hope that helps. I want him to pay for what he did to me. I want him to never be able to hurt me or anyone else again. (my voice cracking) I just want to feel safe again.

"I hear you, Leila, and we are going to do everything in our power to make sure that happens. The fact that his hands were bare is significant—it means we have a much better chance of recovering physical evidence from both the kitchen and your person.

I’m going to step aside for a moment to let the medical team examine you. While they do that, I'm going to have my partner secure the kitchen.

I just have one more thing to ask before I give you some space: Since he's been harassing you, do you know what he's been driving lately, or is there a specific place—like a friend's house or a workplace—where he might have headed?"

(thinking for a moment) Ah, yes... his car. I think he's been driving a black Honda Civic. I've seen him driving it around my neighborhood a few times, and I'm pretty sure it's his. (pausing, trying to remember) As for where he might have headed... (hesitating) There is one place that comes to mind. He has a friend who lives in an apartment complex not too far from here. I've met the friend a few times, and I think his name is Alex. Javier has stayed with him before when we were having problems. (looking up at you) I don't know the exact address, but I can try to describe the apartment complex to you. It's a big place with a pool and a gym... (trailing off, hoping that might be helpful)

(glancing over at the medical team, who are waiting patiently) Oh, I think the medics are waiting for me. (sighing) I'm ready to get this over with. (preparing myself for the examination)

While the medics are looking at your neck, try to think if you remember any part of his license plate—even just a few letters or numbers—but don't stress if you can't. We'll talk again in a few minutes."

(nodding, wincing slightly as the medics examine my neck) Okay, I'll try to think about it. (closing my eyes, concentrating) Hmm... his license plate... (pausing, trying to recall) I think I remember seeing it a few times when he would park his car outside my apartment. (frowning, straining to remember) There was a "J" or a "G" in it, I'm pretty sure. And maybe a "2" or a "5". (shaking my head) I'm sorry, it's not very clear. I don't know why I didn't pay more attention to it at the time. (sighing, opening my eyes) I'm sorry, Officer Davis. I don't know if that helps at all. (looking up at you) Can I ask, will I be able to go home after this? Or do I need to go somewhere else?

narrative:
**INCIDENT REPORT**

**DEPARTMENT:** Rockville Police Department
**DATE OF REPORT:** April 16, 2026
**REPORTING OFFICER:** Officer Davis, Badge #4928
**INCIDENT LOCATION:** 1452 Santiago Drive, Rockville, MD

---

### **INCIDENT CLASSIFICATION**
1. Aggravated Assault (Weapon)
2. Assault by Strangulation
3. Violation of Active Protection Order

### **INVOLVED PARTIES**
* **VICTIM:** Patel, Leila
* **SUSPECT:** Javier (Last Name Unknown at time of report) - Ex-boyfriend of Victim

### **VEHICLE / SUSPECT INFORMATION**
* **VEHICLE:** Honda Civic
* **COLOR:** Black
* **LICENSE PLATE:** Partial plate containing "J" or "G", and "2" or "5"
* **KNOWN ASSOCIATES:** "Alex" (Friend of suspect, resides in a nearby apartment complex featuring a pool and gym).

---

### **NARRATIVE**

On April 16, 2026, I responded to a dispatched call at 1452 Santiago Drive regarding a possible protection order violation and reported harassment. 

Upon arrival, I made contact with the resident and victim, Leila Patel. Ms. Patel was visibly distressed and reported that her ex-boyfriend, identified as Javier, had unlawfully entered her residence earlier this morning in direct violation of an active protection order. 

Ms. Patel stated that the suspect confronted her in an aggressive manner, yelling that she "was his" and "couldn't leave him." The suspect then proceeded to the kitchen and retrieved a weapon. The victim specified that the suspect pulled a large chef's knife from a five-slot wooden knife block located on the kitchen counter adjacent to the sink. 

While holding the knife, the suspect threatened the victim with physical harm if she did not comply with his demands. The suspect then dropped the weapon or used his free hands to grab the victim by the throat, applying pressure and choking her. The victim stated she was unable to breathe during the assault and feared for her life. She confirmed the suspect was not wearing gloves and described his bare hands as feeling "cold and rough." 

The assault concluded when the victim was able to separate from the suspect. She immediately fled to the living room, locked the door, and dialed 911. The victim remained on the line with dispatch until she heard police sirens. She reported hearing the front door slam and footsteps outside, but did not visually witness the suspect's direction of travel or his vehicle. 

**MEDICAL & EVIDENCE:**
Due to the report of strangulation and the victim's complaints of neck pain and pain when swallowing, EMS was dispatched to the scene to conduct a thorough medical evaluation. 

The kitchen area, specifically the counter by the sink and the wooden knife block, was immediately secured as a crime scene. Because the victim confirmed the suspect handled the knife and choked her with bare hands, forensics units were called to process the kitchen surfaces and the victim's person for latent fingerprints and DNA.

**FOLLOW-UP / ACTIONS TAKEN:**
1.  Medical evaluation provided to the victim on the scene by EMS.
2.  Crime scene tape deployed to secure the kitchen pending forensic processing.
3.  A BOLO (Be On the Lookout) was issued to all surrounding units for a black Honda Civic with a partial plate matching the victim's description.
4.  Officers are actively canvas nearby apartment complexes matching the description provided by the victim to locate the suspect's known associate, "Alex."
5.  Victim safety plan discussed, including alternative lodging while the suspect remains at large. 

**STATUS:** Active / Ongoing Investigation. Suspect is currently at large and considered armed and dangerous.

I'm following up on the incident reported earlier today at 1452 Santiago Drive regarding a possible protection order violation involving your ex-boyfriend. I know going over these events can be incredibly stressful, so please take all the time you need. We're just here to get the facts down for the official report. Whenever you're ready, please start from the beginning and walk me through exactly what happened."
0.reasoning: The officer expresses concern for the victim's well-being, which may lead to a discussion about medical attention, but does not directly ask if medical attention is needed.

2.officer_message:
"Ms. Patel, I hear you, and your fear is completely justified. What you just described is an incredibly traumatic and dangerous experience, but I want to assure you that you are safe right now. Because he forced his way in, used a weapon, and choked you, we are looking at much more serious charges than just a protection order violation, including aggravated assault.

To help my team locate him immediately and secure the scene, I need to pin down a few specific facts from you.

First, I strongly advise that we have EMS come in to check your neck. Strangulation can cause severe internal injuries that aren't always immediately visible, and having a medical record will also strengthen the case against him. Please let me know if you will consent to a medical evaluation.

Second, regarding the kitchen knife he threatened you with, I need you to clarify if he brought that weapon with him to your house, or if he grabbed it from your own kitchen.

Finally, please tell me exactly how the assault ended and how he left the property, including whether he fled on foot or if you saw him drive away in a specific vehicle."
2.reasoning: The officer advises the victim to have a medical evaluation and mentions the importance of a medical record for the case, which directly addresses the victim's need for medical attention.

4.officer_message:
"I’m sorry, Leila. I’m moving too fast. Let’s take a second. You’re doing great, and the most important thing is that you’re safe and the medics are on their way to help you.

You mentioned he went into the kitchen to get that knife. Can you point out to me exactly where he grabbed it from? I want to make sure my team doesn't touch that area so we can get his prints off the counter or the drawer."
4.reasoning: The officer mentions medics are on the way to help the victim, which directly addresses the element of medical attention.

8.officer_message:
"I hear you, Leila, and we are going to do everything in our power to make sure that happens. The fact that his hands were bare is significant—it means we have a much better chance of recovering physical evidence from both the kitchen and your person.

I’m going to step aside for a moment to let the medical team examine you. While they do that, I'm going to have my partner secure the kitchen.

I just have one more thing to ask before I give you some space: Since he's been harassing you, do you know what he's been driving lately, or is there a specific place—like a friend's house or a workplace—where he might have headed?"
8.reasoning: The officer mentions the medical team examining the victim, which directly addresses the need for medical attention
